title: Refusing chemo: Just want to vent here because I can’t stop thinking about a convo I had recently.
body:
I went to Pilates the other day and the teacher told me (I am more than halfway through 8 rounds of chemo) that she had cancer 15 years ago. 

She had the tumour removed and just never went back to the hospital for the chemo. Her “faith and positivity” got her through. 

It made me feel so angry to be honest. I don’t know why she bothered telling me - it was like she thought I was an idiot for doing chemo. 

I understand that it’s a personal decision but maybe keep it to yourself if you’re talking to someone in active treatment?

That said, I am intrigued as to why people are refusing chemo - is it solely religion based, or is it fear or a belief that it’s ineffective. 

Personally, I just think this way of thinking is so damaging - luckily I am not that easily swayed (too late now anyway) but some people can be.
